Skip to contents
UtopiaPlanitia 0.4.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Architecture — UtopiaPlanitia

Source: ARCHITECTURE.md

Generated by scriber for run 2026-07-06-loco-drop-conformal on 2026-07-06. Updated by scriber for run 2026-07-07-loco-s3-methods on 2026-07-07. Updated by scriber for run 2026-07-08-kfold-loco on 2026-07-08.

Overview

UtopiaPlanitia is an R package (built on grf) providing variable importance, heterogeneity tests, nuisance-estimation backends, and visualization tools for causal forests. Most functions analyze heterogeneous treatment effect (HTE) estimates from a fitted grf::causal_forest, though loco() and its supporting infrastructure work on plain outcome models (ranger/grf regression, probability, and classification forests) rather than causal forests. The package is organized into three functional clusters — nuisance estimation backends, diagnostics/tests, and visualization — all consumed via S3 dispatch (summary(), plot()) on a fitted forest object. grf and rlang are the only hard Imports; everything else (ggplot2, ranger, glmnet, xgboost, TabPFN, the mlr3 stack, conformalInference-style oracles, etc.) is Suggests and guarded with requireNamespace().

As of the 2026-07-06-loco-drop-conformal run, loco() no longer depends on the non-CRAN conformalInference package for any code path — its one remaining non-trivial split-mode case (per-variable, ranger, regression, loss = "abs") now falls through to the package’s own loco_custom_split() sample-splitting + Z/Wilcoxon inference routine, which already handled every other split-mode case.

As of this run (2026-07-07-loco-s3-methods), loco() returns a classed loco_vimp object ($vimp data frame + scalar metadata) instead of a bare data.frame, and gains print/summary/plot S3 methods in a new R/loco_methods.R, giving it the same S3 dispatch experience cf_loco() and cf_perm() already have. The class is loco_vimp, not the more obvious loco, specifically to avoid colliding with the non-CRAN conformalInference package’s own "loco" class and print.loco() method (used only as a test-only correctness oracle in this package’s test suite).

As of this run (2026-07-08-kfold-loco), loco()’s split-sample mode gains an opt-in K-fold cross-fit path (cross.fit = FALSE, num.folds = 5L, appended trailing formals; the default is bit-identical to before). Instead of a single 50/50 split, every observation contributes to the importance estimate exactly once across num.folds folds, at the cost of num.folds * (p + 1) model refits. Because the per-fold statistics are not independent, inference uses the Nadeau-Bengio (NB) corrected standard error already implemented in cf_perm()’s own cross.fit = TRUE path — extracted into a new shared, reference-parameterized helper, .nb_ttest(Psi, n1, n2, reference = c("t", "z")), called by both loco()’s new .loco_cv() and a refactored cf_perm()’s .cf_perm_cv(). The two callers deliberately diverge on reference: cf_perm() keeps the original t_{K-1} reference (its call site is unchanged, reference defaults to "t"), while loco() explicitly passes reference = "z" — a Monte Carlo simulation study found that the heavy-tailed t_{K-1} critical value, stacked on top of the NB correction’s own (by-design, K-invariant) variance inflation, suppressed loco()’s cross-fit power gain while Type-I error stayed valid either way; switching only the reference distribution (holding the NB SE formula fixed) restored the power gain without loosening Type-I control. See NEWS.md and runs/2026-07-08-kfold-loco in the workspace repo for the full simulation evidence.

Module Structure

%%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%%
graph TD
    subgraph Nuisance["Nuisance-Estimation Backends"]
        AUTOCF["autocf.R — autocf()"]
        GLMCF["glmcf.R — glmcf()"]
        TABCF["tabcf.R — tabcf()"]
        SETUPTOK["setup_tabpfn_token.R"]
    end

    subgraph Diag["Diagnostics & Tests"]
        LOCO["loco.R — loco()"]
        LOCOM["loco_methods.R"]
        CFLOCO["cf_loco.R — cf_loco()"]
        VIMP["compute_vimp.R"]
        CFLOCOM["cf_loco_methods.R"]
        CFPERM["cf_perm.R — cf_perm()"]
        CFPERMM["cf_perm_methods.R"]
        OMNI["omni_hetero.R — omni_hetero()"]
    end

    subgraph Viz["Visualization"]
        PLOTCF["plot.causal_forest.R"]
        SUMMCF["summary.causal_forest.R"]
        PLOTDIAG["plot_diag.R"]
        PLOTPDP["plot_pdp.R — plot_pdp()"]
        PLOTRANK["plot_rank.R — plot_rank()"]
        PLOTSCAT["plot_scatter.R — plot_scatter()"]
        PLOTRATE["plot_rate.R"]
        PLOTBLP["plot_blp.R"]
        PLOTOMNI["plot.omni_hetero.R"]
        UTOPLOT["utopia_plot.R"]
        VISTYLE["vi_plot_style.R"]
    end

    subgraph Core["Package Infra"]
        ZZZ["zzz.R — .onLoad/.onAttach"]
    end

    CFLOCO --> VIMP
    CFLOCO --> CFLOCOM
    LOCO --> LOCOM
    CFPERM --> CFPERMM
    PLOTCF --> PLOTDIAG
    PLOTCF --> PLOTPDP
    PLOTCF --> PLOTSCAT
    PLOTCF --> PLOTRANK
    PLOTOMNI --> PLOTBLP
    PLOTOMNI --> PLOTRATE
    CFLOCOM --> VISTYLE
    CFPERMM --> VISTYLE
    LOCOM --> VISTYLE
    PLOTPDP --> VISTYLE
    CFLOCOM --> UTOPLOT
    CFPERMM --> UTOPLOT
    PLOTDIAG -.optional.-> MLBAL["MLbalance::random_check()"]
    AUTOCF -.optional.-> MLR3["mlr3 stack (AutoTuner)"]
    AUTOCF -.optional.-> DBARTS["dbarts::bart()"]
    AUTOCF -.optional.-> TABPFN["TabPFN / TABPFN_TOKEN"]
    LOCO -.optional.-> SURVIVAL["survival (loco.R helpers)"]
    CFPERM -. shared .nb_ttest() .-> LOCO

    style LOCO fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style LOCOM fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style CFPERM fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff

One unified diagram grouped by the three functional clusters from CLAUDE.md, plus a small “Package Infra” layer for zzz.R. Dashed edges mark optional (Suggests-only) dependencies. LOCO (R/loco.R) and the new LOCOM (R/loco_methods.R) are highlighted blue — this run’s change (loco() now funnels both return sites through a .new_loco() constructor and gains print/summary/plot S3 methods, mirroring CFLOCOM/CFPERMM’s existing pattern for cf_loco()/cf_perm()). The prior run’s change (LOCO’s split-mode code path dropping the conformalInference-calling branch) is also still reflected here. This run (2026-07-08-kfold-loco) adds CFPERM to the changed set (R/cf_perm.R’s .cf_perm_cv() was refactored to call the new shared .nb_ttest() helper that now lives in R/loco.R, marked by the new dashed cross-file edge) and adds LOCO’s opt-in K-fold cross-fit path (.loco_cv() and friends — see the Function Call Graph below). The refactor is provably bit-identical for cf_perm()’s existing behavior (its call site keeps the helper’s default reference = "t"); only loco()’s new cross-fit path uses reference = "z".

Module Reference

Module / File Layer Purpose Key Exports Changed
R/autocf.R Nuisance Auto-selected nuisance causal forest; cross-fits a candidate pool (grf, glmnet, xgboost, tabpfn, bart) and picks the best per nuisance by K-fold weighted MSE autocf() no
R/glmcf.R Nuisance Nuisances via cv.glmnet glmcf() no
R/tabcf.R Nuisance Nuisances via TabPFN tabcf() no
R/setup_tabpfn_token.R Nuisance Sets up the TABPFN_TOKEN env var setup_tabpfn_token() no
R/loco.R Diagnostics LOCO variable importance for ranger/grf outcome forests (regression/probability/classification); auto-detects backend; split-sample and OOB modes; both return sites funnel through the internal .new_loco() constructor to build a classed loco_vimp object. Split-sample mode gains an opt-in K-fold cross-fit path (cross.fit = TRUE, num.folds = 5L): .loco_cv() refits the full + per-target models on num.folds - 1 folds and evaluates on the held-out fold, so every observation contributes once; inference via the shared .nb_ttest() NB-corrected SE, referenced against z (reference = "z") rather than cf_perm()’s t_{K-1} loco() (+ internal loco_custom_split(), .loco_cv(), .loco_assign_folds(), .loco_fit_one(), .loco_predict_one(), .loco_residuals_one(), .nb_ttest(), detect_backend(), oob_loss(), .new_loco(), etc.) yes — return shape changed from a bare data.frame to a classed loco_vimp list ($vimp + metadata) [prior run]; this run adds the cross-fit path (6 new internal functions) plus cross.fit/num.folds metadata fields on .new_loco()
R/loco_methods.R Diagnostics S3 print/summary/plot for loco_vimp objects (class is loco_vimp, not loco, to avoid a clash with conformalInference’s own "loco" class/print.loco()); plot.loco_vimp draws a two-sided CI whisker (unlike cf_perm’s one-sided) and degrades gracefully (no whisker, title gains “(OOB)”) when loco() was called with split = FALSE print.loco_vimp, summary.loco_vimp, plot.loco_vimp yes [prior run — new file]; this run — print.loco_vimp’s header additionally notes “cross-fit” + num.folds when x$cross.fit is TRUE, silent otherwise
R/cf_loco.R + R/compute_vimp.R Diagnostics LOCO variable importance for causal forests cf_loco() no
R/cf_loco_methods.R Diagnostics S3 print/summary/plot for cf_loco objects (S3 methods) no
R/cf_perm.R Diagnostics PermuCATE conditional-permutation variable importance (Paillard et al., 2025). Its own cross.fit = TRUE path (.cf_perm_cv()) is behaviorally unchanged this run — refactored to call the new shared .nb_ttest() (default reference = "t", i.e. t_{K-1}, matching its pre-refactor arithmetic bit-for-bit) instead of computing the NB correction inline cf_perm() yes — .cf_perm_cv()’s inline NB block replaced by a call to the shared .nb_ttest(); verified bit-identical output (no behavior change)
R/cf_perm_methods.R Diagnostics S3 print/summary/plot for cf_perm objects (S3 methods) no
R/omni_hetero.R Diagnostics Omnibus HTE test battery (calibration, high/low CATE, RATE) omni_hetero() no
R/plot.causal_forest.R Visualization S3 plot() dispatcher (type = "diag"/"pdp"/"scatter"/"rank") plot.causal_forest no
R/summary.causal_forest.R Visualization S3 summary() / print.summary() for causal forests summary.causal_forest, print.summary.causal_forest no
R/plot_diag.R Visualization Multi-panel diagnostic plot (optional MLbalance balance panel) (internal panels) no
R/plot_pdp.R Visualization 1-way/2-way partial dependence plots; subgroup = TRUE AIPW mode plot_pdp() no
R/plot_rank.R Visualization Ranked CATEs with CIs plot_rank(), rank_plot() no
R/plot_scatter.R Visualization Individual OOB CATEs vs. a covariate plot_scatter() no
R/plot_rate.R Visualization TOC/RATE curve panel (internal, used by plot.omni_hetero.R) no
R/plot_blp.R Visualization BLP calibration panel (internal, used by plot.omni_hetero.R) no
R/plot.omni_hetero.R Visualization S3 print/plot for omni_hetero objects plot.omni_hetero, print.omni_hetero no
R/utopia_plot.R Visualization Shared S3 print method wrapping plot objects print.utopia_plot no
R/vi_plot_style.R Visualization Shared VI-plot theme/style helpers used by cf_loco/cf_perm/plot_pdp (internal helpers) no
R/zzz.R Infra Package .onLoad/.onAttach hooks (none exported) no

R/old/ is archived/pruned code (not built). dev/ and runs/ are agent/scratch working dirs (build-ignored). tools/blp_smoke.R is a standalone Rscript smoke test for plot_blp.R/omni_hetero.R.

Function Call Graph

%%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%%
graph TD
    loco["loco()"]
    loco --> detect_backend["detect_backend()"]
    loco --> treetype["treetype_for_backend()"]
    loco --> pred_names["pred_names_for()"]
    loco --> extract_hp["extract_grf_hp()"]
    loco --> oob_loss["oob_loss()"]
    loco --> loco_split["loco_custom_split()"]
    loco --> grf_refit["grf_refit()"]
    loco --> new_loco[".new_loco() (constructor)"]
    loco --> loco_cv[".loco_cv() (cross.fit=TRUE)"]

    loco_split --> loco_fit_one[".loco_fit_one()"]
    loco_split --> loco_predict_one[".loco_predict_one()"]
    loco_split --> loco_residuals_one[".loco_residuals_one()"]
    loco_split --> new_loco
    loco_fit_one --> ranger_fit["ranger::ranger()"]
    loco_fit_one --> grf_refit
    loco_residuals_one --> ztest["stats::pnorm/qnorm (method='z')"]
    loco_residuals_one --> wilcoxtest["stats::wilcox.test(exact=FALSE) (method='wilcox')"]

    loco_cv --> loco_assign_folds[".loco_assign_folds()"]
    loco_cv --> loco_fit_one
    loco_cv --> loco_predict_one
    loco_cv --> loco_residuals_one
    loco_cv --> nb_ttest[".nb_ttest(reference='z')"]
    loco_cv --> new_loco
    nb_ttest -. shared helper, reference='t' default .-> cfperm_cv["cf_perm.R: .cf_perm_cv()"]

    oob_loss --> ranger_fit

    new_loco -. returns loco_vimp object .-> print_loco["print.loco_vimp()"]
    new_loco -. returns loco_vimp object .-> summary_loco["summary.loco_vimp()"]
    new_loco -. returns loco_vimp object .-> plot_loco["plot.loco_vimp()"]
    summary_loco --> print_loco
    plot_loco --> vi_xbreaks[".utopia_vi_xbreaks()"]
    plot_loco --> pdp_theme[".utopia_pdp_theme()"]

    style loco fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style loco_split fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style new_loco fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style print_loco fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style summary_loco fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style plot_loco fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style loco_cv fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style loco_assign_folds fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style loco_fit_one fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style loco_predict_one fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style loco_residuals_one fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style nb_ttest fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style cfperm_cv fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff

Blue nodes changed in this run (2026-07-08-kfold-loco), the prior 2026-07-07-loco-s3-methods run, or 2026-07-06-loco-drop-conformal. Both of loco()’s return sites (the OOB loop and the end of loco_custom_split()) funnel through the internal .new_loco() constructor, which builds the classed loco_vimp object consumed by the three S3 methods (print.loco_vimp/summary.loco_vimp/plot.loco_vimp, dotted edges since these are dispatched by the user on the returned object, not called directly by loco()). summary.loco_vimp delegates to print.loco_vimp; plot.loco_vimp reuses the shared .utopia_vi_xbreaks() / .utopia_pdp_theme() helpers already used by plot.cf_loco/plot.cf_perm (no duplicated plotting infrastructure). This run: loco_custom_split()’s three private closures (fit_one/predict_one/residuals_one) were refactored into the standalone, index-parameterized .loco_fit_one()/.loco_predict_one()/ .loco_residuals_one() (verified bit-identical), which are now shared with the new .loco_cv() — the cross-fit path loco() dispatches to when cross.fit = TRUE. .loco_cv() assigns folds via .loco_assign_folds() (stratified by class in probability/classification mode) and aggregates per-fold statistics via .nb_ttest(..., reference = "z"). The dashed cross-file edge shows .nb_ttest() is also called by R/cf_perm.R’s .cf_perm_cv(), which keeps the helper’s default reference = "t" (t_{K-1}, its original behavior, unchanged) — the two callers share the NB standard-error arithmetic but deliberately diverge on which reference distribution the p-value/CI use. From the 2026-07-06 run: loco()’s split-mode branch that used to call the external conformalInference::loco() was deleted; every split-mode case now calls the in-package loco_custom_split(), unmodified in its own inference logic.

Function Reference

Function Defined In Called By Calls Changed Purpose
loco() R/loco.R user / exported detect_backend, treetype_for_backend, pred_names_for, extract_grf_hp, oob_loss, loco_custom_split, .loco_cv, grf_refit, .new_loco yes Public entry point; validates args (incl. new cross.fit/num.folds checks), recovers hyperparameters, dispatches to split-mode (loco_custom_split or, if cross.fit = TRUE, .loco_cv) or OOB mode, returns via .new_loco()
.new_loco() R/loco.R loco() (OOB return site), loco_custom_split() (split return site), .loco_cv() (cross-fit return site) — yes Internal constructor; single funnel point building the classed loco_vimp list ($vimp data frame, sorted descending by Importance, + scalar metadata n/p/method/loss/split/alpha/bonf.correct/backend/group/cross.fit/num.folds)
.loco_cv() R/loco.R loco() (split-mode branch, cross.fit = TRUE only) .loco_assign_folds, .loco_fit_one, .loco_residuals_one, .nb_ttest, .new_loco yes — new K-fold cross-fit procedure: for each fold, fits the full + per-target reduced models on the other folds, evaluates loss-residual differences on the held-out fold (Psi[f, g]), aggregates via NB-corrected, z-referenced inference, builds a two-sided CI
.loco_assign_folds() R/loco.R .loco_cv() — yes — new Assigns num.folds fold labels; stratified by class (sample(rep_len(...)) per level) in probability/classification mode, plain random in regression mode — mirrors cf_perm.R’s .cf_perm_folds()
.loco_fit_one() / .loco_predict_one() / .loco_residuals_one() R/loco.R loco_custom_split() (via thin wrappers, train_idx=i1, test_idx=i2) and .loco_cv() (per fold) ranger::ranger, grf_refit, stats::predict, loss switch (abs/mse/brier/zero_one/log) yes — new (refactored out of loco_custom_split()’s private closures; verified bit-identical) Index-parameterized fit/predict/residual primitives shared by both the single-split and cross-fit code paths
.nb_ttest() R/loco.R .loco_cv() (reference = "z"), R/cf_perm.R’s .cf_perm_cv() (reference = "t", default, call site unchanged) stats::pnorm/stats::pt yes — new (extracted, shared with cf_perm.R) Nadeau-Bengio SE aggregation core (imp, se = sqrt((1/K + rho)*s2), t, zero-SE handling); reference selects only the p-value’s reference distribution, leaving the SE formula identical either way
loco_custom_split() R/loco.R loco() (unconditionally, for every split-mode case when cross.fit = FALSE) .loco_fit_one, .loco_predict_one, .loco_residuals_one (via thin wrappers passing train_idx=i1, test_idx=i2), stats::pnorm/qnorm, stats::wilcox.test, .new_loco no (behavior byte-identical to before; its own three private closures now delegate to the shared standalone functions, verified bit-identical, so this is the back-compat-preserving code path cross.fit = FALSE always takes) Sample-splits data, refits full + per-target reduced models, computes one-sided Z or Wilcoxon inference on loss-residual differences
detect_backend() R/loco.R loco() — no Maps model class to "ranger"/"grf_reg"/"grf_brf"/"grf_prob" or errors
treetype_for_backend() R/loco.R loco() — no Resolves treetype string ("Regression", "Probability estimation", "Classification")
pred_names_for() R/loco.R loco() — no Predictor names in fitted-model column order
extract_grf_hp() R/loco.R loco() — no Recovers grf hyperparameters for refitting
grf_refit() R/loco.R loco(), loco_custom_split() grf::regression_forest/etc. no Refits a grf model with recovered hyperparameters on a data subset
oob_loss() R/loco.R loco() (OOB mode only) — no Computes OOB prediction-error loss for a fitted model
print.loco_vimp() R/loco_methods.R user (via print()/summary() dispatch) — yes Prints the $vimp table (sig stars from fixed p-value buckets, NA-omitted when OOB), header line (n/p/method/loss/mode); this run the header additionally prints “(cross-fit, num.folds = N)” when isTRUE(x$cross.fit), silent (byte-identical to before) otherwise; returns x invisibly
summary.loco_vimp() R/loco_methods.R user (via summary() dispatch) print.loco_vimp yes — new Identical output to print(); returns x invisibly
plot.loco_vimp() R/loco_methods.R user (via plot() dispatch) .utopia_vi_xbreaks, .utopia_pdp_theme yes — new Horizontal VI bar chart, diamond tips, two-sided CI whisker (split mode) or no whisker (OOB), significance fill via p.value < alpha
cf_loco() R/cf_loco.R user / exported compute_vimp no LOCO variable importance for causal forests (separate from loco())

Data Flow

%%{init: {'theme': 'neutral'}}%%
graph TD
    Input["loco(model, data, ..., loss, split, method, cross.fit, num.folds)"] --> Validate["Validate args + detect backend/treetype (incl. cross.fit/num.folds checks)"]
    Validate --> Recover["Recover hyperparameters (hp.args)"]
    Recover --> Mode{{"split = TRUE?"}}
    Mode -- yes --> BuildXY["Build x / y_train matrix"]
    BuildXY --> CFDecision{{"cross.fit = TRUE?"}}
    CFDecision -- no --> CustomSplit["loco_custom_split(): sample-split, refit full + per-target, compute residual deltas"]
    CFDecision -- yes --> CrossFitCV[".loco_cv(): K-fold refit, per-fold residual deltas, NB-corrected z-referenced inference"]
    CustomSplit --> InfMethod{{"method?"}}
    InfMethod -- z --> ZTest["Normal-theory Z-test (pnorm/qnorm)"]
    InfMethod -- wilcox --> WilcoxTest["Wilcoxon signed-rank (exact = FALSE)"]
    ZTest --> Assemble["Assemble output frame"]
    WilcoxTest --> Assemble
    CrossFitCV --> Assemble
    Mode -- no --> OOBLoop["OOB mode: per-target refit + oob_loss() delta"]
    OOBLoop --> Assemble
    Assemble --> NewLoco[".new_loco(): build loco_vimp object"]
    NewLoco --> Output["loco_vimp: $vimp (Variable/Importance/CI.lower/CI.upper/p.value[/Members]) + metadata (n/p/method/loss/split/alpha/bonf.correct/backend/group/cross.fit/num.folds)"]
    Output -.-> Dispatch{{"user calls print()/summary()/plot()"}}

    style CustomSplit fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style NewLoco fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style CFDecision fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff
    style CrossFitCV fill:#1e90ff,stroke:#1565c0,color:#fff

Vertical flowchart. The use_conformal branch that used to sit between “Build x / y_train matrix” and an external conformalInference::loco() call has been removed — every split-mode path flows through either loco_custom_split() (the unchanged, back-compat-preserving default) or, as of this run (2026-07-08-kfold-loco), the new .loco_cv() cross-fit path when cross.fit = TRUE is requested (highlighted decision diamond and node) — both rejoin at “Assemble output frame” immediately, keeping the branch narrow. Both the split-mode and OOB paths converge on the .new_loco() constructor (highlighted) before returning, producing a classed loco_vimp object instead of a bare sorted data.frame; that object is then available for print()/summary()/plot() dispatch (dotted edge — an optional, user-initiated step, not part of loco()’s own execution). Two early errors (not shown as separate nodes to keep the chain narrow) short-circuit before “Validate” completes whenever cross.fit = TRUE is combined with method = "wilcox" or split = FALSE.

Architectural Patterns

  • S3 dispatch is the public interface. A fitted causal_forest flows into summary() / plot(); LOCO objects (cf_loco, and now loco_vimp) and test objects (omni_hetero) carry their own print/summary/plot methods. As of this run, loco() (which operates on outcome forests, not causal forests) follows the same pattern as cf_loco()/cf_perm(): it returns a classed loco_vimp list ($vimp + metadata) rather than a bare data.frame, with print.loco_vimp/summary.loco_vimp/plot.loco_vimp methods in R/loco_methods.R. The class is loco_vimp rather than the more obvious loco specifically to avoid an S3-method collision with the non-CRAN conformalInference package’s own "loco" class (a test-only correctness-oracle dependency in this package’s test suite, never a runtime dependency) — see NEWS.md and runs/2026-07-07-loco-s3-methods in the workspace repo for the discovery and resolution.
  • Guarded optional dependencies. grf and rlang are the only hard imports; every other package (ggplot2, ranger, glmnet, xgboost, dbarts, the mlr3 stack, TabPFN, MLbalance, survival, etc.) is Suggests and guarded with requireNamespace(), so features degrade gracefully when a suggested package is absent. As of this run, conformalInference (a non-CRAN, GitHub-only package) has been fully removed from this guarded-dependency set — it is no longer imported, required, or referenced anywhere in R/, DESCRIPTION, or generated docs.
  • RNG lockstep between alternative implementations of the same statistical procedure. loco_custom_split()’s sample-split (set.seed(seed); sample(seq_len(n), floor(n/2))) and Z-test formula are algebraically identical to what the removed conformalInference::loco() branch used to compute, because ranger::ranger() (when not given an explicit seed=) draws exactly one RNG value per call from R’s global stream — so two code paths performing the same sequence of sample() + ranger() calls from the same seed produce bit-identical fitted forests and downstream statistics. This is why the migration in this run produced numerically identical method = "z" output (see log-entry.md for the full verification record).
  • Dependency-free-by-construction internal helpers. loco_custom_split() is a single, self-contained internal function reused across every ranger/grf x per-variable/group x regression/probability/classification combination in split mode — new backends or loss functions extend it rather than adding parallel branches.
  • Shared statistical-inference core, parameterized by reference distribution, not duplicated per caller. As of this run (2026-07-08-kfold-loco), the Nadeau-Bengio corrected-SE arithmetic that cross-fitting requires (shared between cf_perm()’s pre-existing cross.fit = TRUE path and loco()’s new one) lives in exactly one place, .nb_ttest(Psi, n1, n2, reference = c("t", "z")) in R/loco.R, called by both cf_perm.R’s .cf_perm_cv() (default reference = "t", its original, unchanged behavior) and loco.R’s .loco_cv() (reference = "z", deliberately divergent). The standard-error formula — the part that must never drift between the two callers — is structurally impossible to diverge, since it is computed once inside the shared function regardless of reference; only the one-sided p-value’s (and, by each caller, the CI half-width’s) reference distribution can vary. This is the same “one algorithm, index/parameter-threaded, reused by multiple call sites” pattern loco_custom_split()’s own refactor (see above) already established for the fit/predict/residual primitives — applied here to an inference core shared across two sibling exported functions rather than within one.

Notes

  • This run (2026-07-06-loco-drop-conformal) removed the sole conformalInference-calling branch in loco(); the package now has zero code paths that reference conformalInference anywhere, and it has been dropped from DESCRIPTION Suggests. This is documented as an accepted, narrowly-scoped breaking change (numeric output on the migrated path is exact for method = "z", within an empirically-calibrated tolerance for method = "wilcox") — see NEWS.md under “Breaking changes” and log-entry.md for the full verification evidence.
  • ARCHITECTURE.md did not exist in this repo prior to this run; this is the first version. Future runs should treat this file as living documentation and update the relevant diagram/table rather than regenerating it wholesale, unless a change is broad enough to warrant a full rebuild.
  • R/loco.R’s verbose argument is retained (never removed — see the package’s “add, don’t change; deprecate, don’t delete” API contract in CLAUDE.md) but is now a documented inert no-op on every code path; it previously only ever controlled progress printing inside the now-removed conformalInference branch.
  • This run (2026-07-07-loco-s3-methods) added R/loco_methods.R and changed loco()’s return shape from a bare data.frame to a classed loco_vimp list, via a new internal .new_loco() constructor funneling both existing return sites (OOB loop, end of loco_custom_split()). This is not a breaking change: loco() has never been released (zero git tags reference it), so its return shape carries no backwards-compatibility obligation yet. loco()’s own exported name, formals, defaults, and argument order are completely unchanged (T-API: loco formals unchanged guard passes unmodified). The .Rbuildignore entry ^ARCHITECTURE\.md$ was added in this run, clearing the pre-existing R CMD check NOTE (“Non-standard file/directory found at top level: ‘ARCHITECTURE.md’”) that every prior run’s audit.md had flagged as present-but-non-blocking.
  • This run (2026-07-08-kfold-loco) added an opt-in K-fold cross-fit path to loco()’s split-sample mode (cross.fit = FALSE, num.folds = 5L, two new trailing formals; the default reproduces prior output bit-for-bit, verified across 7 scenarios and the full pre-existing test suite). Six new internal functions were added to R/loco.R (.loco_cv, .loco_assign_folds, .loco_fit_one, .loco_predict_one, .loco_residuals_one, .nb_ttest), and R/cf_perm.R’s .cf_perm_cv() was refactored (not rewritten) to call the new shared .nb_ttest() instead of computing the NB correction inline — verified bit-identical to cf_perm()’s pre-refactor output. No new exported symbol, no new package dependency, DESCRIPTION/NAMESPACE byte-identical to the pre-feature commit. A dedicated Monte Carlo simulation study (7 cells, ~2,700 loco() calls, documented in runs/2026-07-08-kfold-loco/simulation.md in the workspace repo) found the feature’s Type-I error control was clean but its initial t_{K-1}-referenced inference over-suppressed the power/ data-efficiency gain the feature is meant to deliver. A follow-up investigation (same simulation artifact’s “Investigation” section) root-caused this to the heavy-tailed t_{K-1} critical value stacking on top of the NB correction’s own (K-invariant, by-design) variance inflation, and validated a fix: reference the same NB-corrected SE against the standard normal (z) instead, holding the SE formula fixed. This was implemented as the reference argument on .nb_ttest() (default "t", preserving cf_perm()’s behavior unchanged; loco()’s .loco_cv() explicitly requests "z") rather than as two separate helper functions, so the SE arithmetic cannot drift between the two callers. Both the original feature and the z-reference follow-up are captured in this repo’s git history as separate commits (99e121c then c852061, both now part of feat/kfold-loco); see NEWS.md and log-entry.md (runs/2026-07-08-kfold-loco in the workspace repo) for the full simulation evidence and the decision record.

On this page

Developed by Jack T. Rametta.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
